data.title: Stocks Fall, GM Eyes Best Day In A Year, Gold Breaks Records, Silver Hits 12-Year Highs: What's Driving Markets Tuesday?
data.description:
Wall Street traded lower for the second consecutive day on Tuesday, as investor sentiment turned cautious with the U.S. presidential election just two weeks away. 



Concerns over U.S. fiscal policies and potential shifts in economic direction fueled the pause in risk-taking.

data.title: With FDA Approval iRhythm May Be Able To Lift Regulatory Overhang, Investor Skepticism, Analyst Says
data.description: FDA approves iRhythm's 510(k) submission for Zio AT device. A second approval may come soon, enhancing the company's mobile cardiac telemetry services.

data.title: Coca-Cola Q3 Earnings Preview: Can Beverage Giant Keep EPS, Revenue Beat Streaks Alive?
data.description: Coca Cola reports Q3 results Oct. 23. A look at the key figures from analysts and items investors should be watching in the report.

data.title: CrowdStrike And Fortinet Partner To Supercharge Cybersecurity: Details
data.description: The CrowdStrike and Fortinet partnership enhances network and endpoint security while simplifying operations and improving threat detection and response.

data.title: Boeing Stock Braces For Bumpy Q3 Landing
data.description: Boeing stock to report Q3 earnings with $1.49 loss per share and $18.6B revenue. Investors hope for recovery from production issues and delivery delays.
